get_block(List, Start, Length) ->
    lists:sublist(List, Start, Length).

% Helper to check if B is a sublist of A. This implementation is O(|A|*|B|).
is_sublist(B, A) when lists:length(B) == 0 ->
    true;
is_sublist(B, A) when lists:length(A) < lists:length(B) ->
    false;
is_sublist(B, A) ->
    lists:any(fun(Start -> 
        lists:lists:==(lists:sublist(A, Start, lists:length(B)), B)
    ), 
    lists:seq(0, lists:length(A) - lists:length(B) + 1)).

% Helper to check if B is a cyclic shift of A. O(|A|^2).
is_cyclic_shift(A, B) when lists:length(A) /= lists:length(B) ->
    false;
is_cyclic_shift(A, B) when lists:length(A) == 0 ->
    true;
is_cyclic_shift(A, B) ->
    A_doubled = A ++ A,
    is_sublist(B, A_doubled).

% Checks if all blocks of size K in Nums can be cyclically shifted to match the blocks of S.
check_k(Nums, N, K) ->
    S = lists:sort(Nums),
    M = N div K,
    check_blocks_recursive(S, Nums, M, K, 0).

check_blocks_recursive(_, _, M, _, _) when M <= 0 ->
    true;
check_blocks_recursive(S, Nums, M, K, CurrentIndex) ->
    BlockSize = K,
    
    Block_S = get_block(S, CurrentIndex, BlockSize),
    Block_Nums = get_block(Nums, CurrentIndex, BlockSize),

    if is_cyclic_shift(Block_Nums, Block_S) ->
        check_blocks_recursive(S, Nums, M, K, CurrentIndex + K);
    true ->
        false.

% Finds all divisors of N.
find_divisors(N) ->
    Divisors = lists:foldl(fun(Acc, D) -> 
        if rem(N, D) == 0 -> 
            lists:insert(N div D, Acc) ; 
            Acc
    end, [], lists:seq(1, floor(sqrt(N)))),
    lists:sort(Divisors).

solve(Nums) ->
    N = lists:length(Nums),
    Divisors = find_divisors(N),
    
    % Filter divisors K where check_k(Nums, N, K) is true, then sum them up.
    ValidK = lists:filter(fun(K -> check_k(Nums, N, K)), Divisors),
    lists:sum(ValidK).

% Helper function to find all divisors of N
divisors(N) ->
    List = [1..N],
    lists:filter(fun(D) -> N remop D == 0 end, List).

% Helper function to check if A is a cyclic permutation of B
% Assumes length(A) == length(B)
check_cyclic(A, B) ->
    K = length(A),
    if K == 0 -> true;
    K == 1 -> A = [X], B = [Y], X == Y;
    true ->
        % Try all K possible rotations of B
        fun(Shift) when Shift >= 0 and Shift < K ->
            RotatedB = lists:rotate(B, Shift),
            A == RotatedB
        end({Shift}, 0, K-1)
    end.

% Helper function to check the condition for a specific k
check_k(Nums, SortedNums, K, N) ->
    M = N div K,
    
    % Check M blocks
    fun(Index) when Index >= 0 and Index < M ->
        Start = Index * K,
        
        % Extract block Bi from Nums
        Bi = lists:sublist(Nums, Start, K),
        
        % Extract block Si from SortedNums
        Si = lists:sublist(SortedNums, Start, K),
        
        % Check if Bi is a cyclic shift of Si
        if not check_cyclic(Bi, Si) ->
            false
        else
            true
        end
    end({Index}, 0, M-1).

% Helper function to rotate a list left by 'shift' positions
% E.g., rotate([1, 2, 3], 1) -> [2, 3, 1]
lists:rotate(List, Shift) ->
    K = length(List),
    Shift = Shift remop K,
    Suffix = lists:sublist(List, Shift, K - Shift),
    Prefix = lists:sublist(List, 0, Shift),
    Suffix ++ Prefix.

% Helper function to get a sublist (slice)

lists:sublist(List, StartIndex, Length) ->

fun solve(Nums) -> integer() ->
    N = length(Nums),
    SortedNums = lists:sort(Nums),
    
    Divisors = divisors(N),
    
    SumK = lists:foldl(fun(Acc, K) ->
        if check_k(Nums, SortedNums, K, N) ->
            Acc + K
        else
            Acc
        end, 0, Divisors),
        
    SumK.

% Helper function to find all divisors of N
divisors(N) ->
    List = [1..N],
    lists:filter(fun(D) -> N remop D == 0 end, List).

% Helper function to check if A is a cyclic permutation of B
% Assumes length(A) == length(B)
check_cyclic(A, B) ->
    K = length(A),
    if K == 0 -> true;
    K == 1 -> A = [X], B = [Y], X == Y;
    true ->
        % Try all K possible rotations of B
        fun(Shift) when Shift >= 0 and Shift < K ->
            RotatedB = lists:rotate(B, Shift),
            A == RotatedB
        end({Shift}, 0, K-1)
    end.

% Helper function to check the condition for a specific k
check_k(Nums, SortedNums, K, N) ->
    M = N div K,
    
    % Check M blocks
    fun(Index) when Index >= 0 and Index < M ->
        Start = Index * K,
        
        % Extract block Bi from Nums
        Bi = lists:sublist(Nums, Start, K),
        
        % Extract block Si from SortedNums
        Si = lists:sublist(SortedNums, Start, K),
        
        % Check if Bi is a cyclic shift of Si
        if not check_cyclic(Bi, Si) ->
            false
        else
            true
        end
    end({Index}, 0, M-1).

% Helper function to rotate a list left by 'shift' positions
% E.g., rotate([1, 2, 3], 1) -> [2, 3, 1]
lists:rotate(List, Shift) ->
    K = length(List),
    Shift = Shift remop K,
    Suffix = lists:sublist(List, Shift, K - Shift),
    Prefix = lists:sublist(List, 0, Shift),
    Suffix ++ Prefix.

% Helper function to get a sublist (slice)
% lists:sublist(List, StartIndex, Length)
lists:sublist(List, StartIndex, Length) ->
    lists:nth_slice(StartIndex, Length, List).
